heading: Fix the Immediate Problem
body:
Consider a few possible actions to help resolve
these alerts:

- [Optimize your queries](manual/core/query-optimization).

- Use the Atlas Performance Advisor
  to view slow queries and suggested [indexes](manual/indexes).

- Review [Indexing Strategies](manual/applications/indexes) for
  possible further indexing improvements.

  > **Note:** You might need to temporarily increase your cluster IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second) to
  > create new indexes. Provisioned IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second) is supported only for
  > `M30+` tier AWS (Amazon Web Services) clusters.

To change a cluster's IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second), go to the
**Cluster Configuration** page and do the following possible
actions:

| Cloud Provider            | Tier          | Possible Actions                                                 |
| ------------------------- | ------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- |
| AWS (Amazon Web Services) | M10, M20      | Increase Storage CapacityScale Cluster Tier                      |
| AWS (Amazon Web Services) | M30 or larger | Increase Cluster IOPSIncrease Storage CapacityScale Cluster Tier |
| Google Cloud              | M10 or larger | Increase Storage CapacityScale Cluster Tier                      |
| Azure                     | M10 or larger | Increase Storage CapacityScale Cluster Tier                      |

- [Analyze Query Performance](manual/tutorial/analyze-query-plan)
  to review how your queries are using your indexes.

- Increase hardware resources, such as instance size and IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second), in the
  Cluster Configuration Page.
